concerns.text: T014 and T016 introduce a 'fallback to text embedding similarity' if FlavorDB is missing. FR-004 and Constitution Principle VI strictly mandate 'FlavorDB chemical vectors' for flavor-profile similarity. A text-embedding fallback is a semantic weakening of the requirement, as text embeddings correlate with frequency (violating Statistical Independence) and are not the mandated chemical proxy. The task must hard-fail or exclude the pair, not substitute a different data source.

T022 and T040 instruct the system to 'flag' the model if VIF > 5 but explicitly 'do NOT drop' the predictor. The Plan (Phase 2 Step 3) and Constitution Principle VI (Statistical Independence) require that multicollinearity be resolved (by dropping or re-specifying) to ensure valid coefficient interpretation. Proceeding with a flagged, collinear model violates the constraint that predictors must be independent for the LRT to be valid.

T008 schedules Power Analysis to run *after* data download (T013) on a sample. The Plan (Phase 0.5) and Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) require sample size (N) to be determined *a priori* to define the down-sampling strategy. Running power analysis post-hoc on a sample violates the constraint of pre-defined experimental design and risks data leakage or biased sample selection.

T030 specifies testing for a 'meaningful positive difference' (delta > 0). SC-002 explicitly requires a hypothesis test for a delta **≥ 0.05**. Testing only for > 0 is a relaxation of the success criterion, as a delta of 0.01 would pass T030 but fail SC-002. The task must enforce the specific 0.05 threshold.

T059 allows the reproducibility audit to 'exclude data download if already cached'. Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) mandates that 'External datasets MUST be fetched from the same canonical source on every run'. Skipping the fetch step violates the non-negotiable reproducibility constraint, as cached data may drift or be corrupted without verification against the canonical source.
